text.en: Make something people want.
text.zh: 做出人們想要的東西。

context.en: Y Combinator's motto; coined about a month after YC started in 2005.
context.zh: Y Combinator 的座右銘;在 2005 年 YC 成立約一個月後提出。

text.en: One of the most common types of advice we give at Y Combinator is to do things that don't scale.
text.zh: 我們在 Y Combinator 最常給的建議之一,就是去做那些無法規模化的事。

context.en: Essay "Do Things that Don't Scale," July 2013.
context.zh: 出自散文〈Do Things that Don't Scale〉,2013 年 7 月。

text.en: Startups don't win by attacking. They win by transcending.
text.zh: 新創不是靠攻擊取勝,而是靠超越取勝。

context.en: Essay "How to Make Wealth," from Hackers & Painters (2004).
context.zh: 出自散文〈How to Make Wealth〉,收錄於《Hackers & Painters》(2004)。

text.en: The most important thing for a startup is to make something people want.
text.zh: 對一家新創來說,最重要的事就是做出人們想要的東西。

context.en: Essay "Startups in 13 Sentences," February 2009.
context.zh: 出自散文〈Startups in 13 Sentences〉,2009 年 2 月。

text.en: It's better to have 100 people love you than to have a million people sort of like you.
text.zh: 讓 100 個人愛你,勝過讓一百萬個人只是有點喜歡你。

context.en: Quoting Brian Chesky / a principle learned from Paul Buchheit; widely cited YC advice.
context.zh: 引述 Brian Chesky 與源自 Paul Buchheit 的原則;YC 廣為流傳的建議。

text.en: A startup is a company designed to grow fast.
text.zh: 新創就是一家為了快速成長而設計的公司。

context.en: Essay "Startup = Growth," September 2012.
context.zh: 出自散文〈Startup = Growth〉,2012 年 9 月。

text.en: The way to get startup ideas is not to try to think of startup ideas. It's to look for problems, preferably problems you have yourself.
text.zh: 想出新創點子的方法,不是硬去想點子,而是去尋找問題,最好是你自己也有的問題。

context.en: Essay "How to Get Startup Ideas," November 2012.
context.zh: 出自散文〈How to Get Startup Ideas〉,2012 年 11 月。

text.en: Live in the future, then build what's missing.
text.zh: 活在未來,然後把缺的東西做出來。

context.en: Essay "How to Get Startup Ideas," November 2012.
context.zh: 出自散文〈How to Get Startup Ideas〉,2012 年 11 月。

text.en: It's better to build something that a small number of users love than a large number of users like.
text.zh: 做出讓少數人深愛的東西,勝過做出讓多數人只是喜歡的東西。

context.en: Stanford CS183B / "How to Start a Startup" lecture, 2014.
context.zh: 出自史丹佛 CS183B「How to Start a Startup」課程,2014 年。

text.en: It's much easier to expand from something that a small number of people love to something that a lot of people love than from something a lot of people like.
text.zh: 從少數人深愛的東西,擴展成多數人深愛的東西,遠比從多數人只是喜歡的東西去擴展來得容易。

context.en: Stanford CS183B / "How to Start a Startup" lecture, 2014.
context.zh: 出自史丹佛 CS183B「How to Start a Startup」課程,2014 年。

text.en: The degree to which the outcome of a startup depends on luck is something most founders underestimate.
text.zh: 新創的成敗有多大程度取決於運氣,是大多數創辦人都低估的。

context.en: Stanford CS183B lecture 1, "Ideas, Products, Teams and Execution," 2014.
context.zh: 出自史丹佛 CS183B 第一講〈Ideas, Products, Teams and Execution〉,2014 年。

text.en: So all you need is a great idea, a great team, a great product, and great execution. So easy!
text.zh: 所以你只需要一個好點子、一支好團隊、一個好產品,加上絕佳的執行力。多麼簡單!

context.en: Stanford CS183B lecture 1, 2014 (said wryly).
context.zh: 出自史丹佛 CS183B 第一講,2014 年(語帶反諷)。

text.en: A bad team will not deliver a good product, ever.
text.zh: 糟糕的團隊永遠做不出好產品。

context.en: Stanford CS183B lecture 1, 2014.
context.zh: 出自史丹佛 CS183B 第一講,2014 年。

text.en: You get rich by owning things that increase rapidly in value.
text.zh: 你致富的方式,是擁有那些價值快速上升的東西。

context.en: Blog post "How to Be Successful," January 2019.
context.zh: 出自部落格文章〈How to Be Successful〉,2019 年 1 月。

text.en: Almost always, the people who say "I am going to keep going until this works, and no matter what the challenges are I'm going to figure them out" win.
text.zh: 幾乎每一次,那些說「我會一直做到它成功為止,不管遇到什麼挑戰我都會想辦法解決」的人,最後都會勝出。

context.en: Blog post "How to Be Successful," January 2019 (on willfulness).
context.zh: 出自部落格文章〈How to Be Successful〉,2019 年 1 月(談意志力)。

text.en: It's better to make a few people really happy than to make a lot of people semi-happy.
text.zh: 讓少數人真正開心,勝過讓很多人半開心。

context.en: Gmail creator's advice popularized via YC; "build something 100 people love."
context.zh: Gmail 之父經 YC 廣傳的建議:「做出 100 個人愛的東西。」

text.en: Limited resources free you to do what's important, instead of trying to do everything.
text.zh: 有限的資源反而讓你能專注做重要的事,而不是想把每件事都做。

context.en: Blog post "Communicating with code," Paul Buchheit's blog.
context.zh: 出自 Paul Buchheit 部落格文章〈Communicating with code〉。

text.en: If your product is great, it doesn't need to be good.
text.zh: 如果你的產品夠卓越,它就不需要面面俱到。

context.en: Blog post "If your product is Great, it doesn't need to be Good," 2010.
context.zh: 出自部落格文章〈If your product is Great, it doesn't need to be Good〉,2010 年。

text.en: Talk to your users and build what they want.
text.zh: 去和你的使用者對話,做出他們真正想要的東西。

context.en: "YC's Essential Startup Advice," Y Combinator.
context.zh: 出自 Y Combinator〈YC's Essential Startup Advice〉。

text.en: Launch something bad quickly, then iterate. Launching a mediocre product and then talking to customers and iterating is much better than waiting to build the perfect product.
text.zh: 先快速推出一個不完美的版本,再持續迭代。推出一個普通的產品,然後和顧客對話、不斷迭代,遠比等著打造「完美產品」更好。

context.en: YC startup advice on launching early; YC Startup Library.
context.zh: YC 關於儘早推出產品的建議;出自 YC Startup Library。

text.en: The number one job of a startup is to build product and talk to users.
text.zh: 新創的首要任務就是打造產品,並且和使用者對話。

context.en: "Building Product," YC Startup Library lecture.
context.zh: 出自 YC Startup Library 課程〈Building Product〉。

text.en: The founders who win are the ones who are still standing after everyone else has given up.
text.zh: 最後勝出的創辦人,是那些在別人都放棄後仍然撐著沒倒下的人。

context.en: On founder resilience; YC talks and interviews.
context.zh: 談創辦人的韌性;出自 YC 演講與訪談。

text.en: Founders who keep going are the ones who do well. It's not about being the smartest—it's about being relentlessly resourceful.
text.zh: 持續堅持下去的創辦人才會做得好。重點不在於最聰明,而在於不屈不撓、想盡辦法解決問題。

context.en: On founder traits as YC President & CEO (since 2023).
context.zh: 身為 YC 總裁兼執行長(2023 年起),談創辦人特質。

text.en: Investors are people too. They get excited about the same things that get customers excited.
text.zh: 投資人也是人。讓顧客興奮的東西,同樣會讓投資人興奮。

context.en: Advice on fundraising and storytelling, from YC and Initialized days.
context.zh: 關於募資與說故事的建議,出自 YC 與 Initialized 時期。

text.en: What I learned is that great founders are relentlessly resourceful, and that's something you can see very early.
text.zh: 我學到的是,優秀的創辦人總能不屈不撓、想盡辦法解決問題,而這在很早期就能看出來。

context.en: Reflecting on years of YC investing; interviews, 2023–2024.
context.zh: 回顧多年 YC 投資經驗;出自訪談,2023–2024 年。

text.en: We funded people we wished someone had funded us when we were starting out.
text.zh: 我們投資的,正是那些當年我們創業時,也希望有人願意投資的人。

context.en: YC co-founder, on the firm's founding philosophy.
context.zh: YC 共同創辦人,談公司的創立理念。

text.en: Determination is the single most important quality in startup founders.
text.zh: 決心是新創創辦人最重要的單一特質。

context.en: Talk "The Importance of Being Determined," 2010.
context.zh: 出自演講〈The Importance of Being Determined〉,2010 年。

text.en: When we read applications, what we're really looking for is determination and a willingness to keep going no matter what.
text.zh: 我們在審閱申請時,真正在尋找的,是那種無論如何都不放棄、堅持到底的決心。

context.en: On evaluating YC applicants; "The Importance of Being Determined," 2010.
context.zh: 談評估 YC 申請者;出自〈The Importance of Being Determined〉,2010 年。
